name: United States v. Wick
summary: A federal case holding that a parts kit designed to facilitate illegal machine gun construction qualified as a regulated weapon under federal law, establishing that using parts kits to evade firearms regulations is not a novel theory invented by the 2022 ATF rule.
significance: The government cited Wick alongside Stewart as another example of pre-rule case law treating weapon parts kits as regulated firearms, reinforcing that the ATF rule's approach has judicial support predating the modern ghost gun era.
